Interesting! The segmentation shows the same nodes as before. The pink bar (Node 0) changed from 63 to 61 pixels, and the charcoal corner (Node 1) changed from 1 to 3 pixels. But the main objects (black, yellow, charcoal, yellow) are the same.
Wait, let me compare:
Before: Node 0: P, 63 pixels, boundary [[0, 0], [0, 62]]
After: Node 0: color=P, pixels=61, boundary=[[0, 0], [0, 60]]
Before: Node 1: color=c, 1 pixel, boundary=[[0, 63]]
After: Node 1: color=c, pixels=3, boundary=[[0, 61], [0, 63]]
So the pink bar shrunk by 2 pixels and the charcoal corner grew by 2 pixels. This suggests the top bar is a timer/progress bar that changes when I click.
But the main objects haven't changed. So clicking on the yellow rectangle didn't affect the main puzzle objects.
